speaker: KENT
text: Here is the place, my lord. Good my lord, enter.

speaker: KENT
text: The tyranny of the open night ’s too rough

speaker: KENT
text: For nature to endure.

speaker: LEAR
text: Let me alone.

speaker: KENT
text: Good my lord, enter here.

speaker: LEAR
text: Wilt break my heart?

speaker: KENT
text: I had rather break mine own. Good my lord, enter.

speaker: LEAR
text: Thou think’st ’tis much that this contentious storm

speaker: LEAR
text: Invades us to the skin. So ’tis to thee.

speaker: LEAR
text: But where the greater malady is fixed,

speaker: LEAR
text: The lesser is scarce felt. Thou ’dst shun a bear,

speaker: LEAR
text: But if thy flight lay toward the roaring sea,

speaker: LEAR
text: Thou ’dst meet the bear i’ th’ mouth. When the mind’s free,

speaker: LEAR
text: The body’s delicate. This tempest in my mind

speaker: LEAR
text: Doth from my senses take all feeling else

speaker: LEAR
text: Save what beats there. Filial ingratitude!

speaker: LEAR
text: Is it not as this mouth should tear this hand

speaker: LEAR
text: For lifting food to ’t? But I will punish home.

speaker: LEAR
text: No, I will weep no more. In such a night

speaker: LEAR
text: To shut me out? Pour on. I will endure.

speaker: LEAR
text: In such a night as this? O Regan, Goneril,

speaker: LEAR
text: Your old kind father whose frank heart gave all!

speaker: LEAR
text: O, that way madness lies. Let me shun that;

speaker: LEAR
text: No more of that.

speaker: KENT
text: Good my lord, enter here.

speaker: LEAR
text: Prithee, go in thyself. Seek thine own ease.

speaker: LEAR
text: This tempest will not give me leave to ponder

speaker: LEAR
text: On things would hurt me more. But I’ll go in.—

speaker: LEAR
text: In, boy; go first.—You houseless poverty—

speaker: LEAR
text: Nay, get thee in. I’ll pray, and then I’ll sleep.

speaker: LEAR
text: Poor naked wretches, wheresoe’er you are,

speaker: LEAR
text: That bide the pelting of this pitiless storm,

speaker: LEAR
text: How shall your houseless heads and unfed sides,

speaker: LEAR
text: Your looped and windowed raggedness defend you

speaker: LEAR
text: From seasons such as these? O, I have ta’en

speaker: LEAR
text: Too little care of this. Take physic, pomp.

speaker: LEAR
text: Expose thyself to feel what wretches feel,

speaker: LEAR
text: That thou may’st shake the superflux to them

speaker: LEAR
text: And show the heavens more just.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Fathom and half, fathom and half!

speaker: EDGAR
text: Poor Tom!

speaker: FOOL
text: Come not in here, nuncle; here’s a spirit. Help

speaker: FOOL
text: me, help me!

speaker: KENT
text: Give me thy hand. Who’s there?

speaker: FOOL
text: A spirit, a spirit! He says his name’s Poor Tom.

speaker: KENT
text: What art thou that dost grumble there i’ th’

speaker: KENT
text: straw? Come forth.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Away. The foul fiend follows me. Through the

speaker: EDGAR
text: sharp hawthorn blows the cold wind. Hum! Go to

speaker: EDGAR
text: thy cold bed and warm thee.

speaker: LEAR
text: Didst thou give all to thy daughters? And art thou

speaker: LEAR
text: come to this?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Who gives anything to Poor Tom, whom the

speaker: EDGAR
text: foul fiend hath led through fire and through flame,

speaker: EDGAR
text: through ford and whirlpool, o’er bog and quagmire;

speaker: EDGAR
text: that hath laid knives under his pillow and

speaker: EDGAR
text: halters in his pew, set ratsbane by his porridge,

speaker: EDGAR
text: made him proud of heart to ride on a bay trotting

speaker: EDGAR
text: horse over four-inched bridges to course his own

speaker: EDGAR
text: shadow for a traitor? Bless thy five wits! Tom’s

speaker: EDGAR
text: a-cold. O, do de, do de, do de. Bless thee from

speaker: EDGAR
text: whirlwinds, star-blasting, and taking! Do Poor Tom

speaker: EDGAR
text: some charity, whom the foul fiend vexes. There

speaker: EDGAR
text: could I have him now, and there—and there again

speaker: EDGAR
text: —and there.

speaker: LEAR
text: Has his daughters brought him to this pass?—

speaker: LEAR
text: Couldst thou save nothing? Wouldst thou give ’em all?

speaker: FOOL
text: Nay, he reserved a blanket, else we had been all

speaker: FOOL
text: shamed.

speaker: LEAR
text: Now all the plagues that in the pendulous air

speaker: LEAR
text: Hang fated o’er men’s faults light on thy daughters!

speaker: KENT
text: He hath no daughters, sir.

speaker: LEAR
text: Death, traitor! Nothing could have subdued nature

speaker: LEAR
text: To such a lowness but his unkind daughters.

speaker: LEAR
text: Is it the fashion that discarded fathers

speaker: LEAR
text: Should have thus little mercy on their flesh?

speaker: LEAR
text: Judicious punishment! ’Twas this flesh begot

speaker: LEAR
text: Those pelican daughters.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Pillicock sat on Pillicock Hill. Alow, alow, loo,

speaker: EDGAR
text: loo.

speaker: FOOL
text: This cold night will turn us all to fools and

speaker: FOOL
text: madmen.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Take heed o’ th’ foul fiend. Obey thy parents,

speaker: EDGAR
text: keep thy word’s justice, swear not, commit not with

speaker: EDGAR
text: man’s sworn spouse, set not thy sweet heart on

speaker: EDGAR
text: proud array. Tom’s a-cold.

speaker: LEAR
text: What hast thou been?

speaker: EDGAR
text: A servingman, proud in heart and mind, that

speaker: EDGAR
text: curled my hair, wore gloves in my cap, served the

speaker: EDGAR
text: lust of my mistress’ heart and did the act of

speaker: EDGAR
text: darkness with her, swore as many oaths as I spake

speaker: EDGAR
text: words and broke them in the sweet face of heaven;

speaker: EDGAR
text: one that slept in the contriving of lust and waked to

speaker: EDGAR
text: do it. Wine loved I deeply, dice dearly, and in

speaker: EDGAR
text: woman out-paramoured the Turk. False of heart,

speaker: EDGAR
text: light of ear, bloody of hand; hog in sloth, fox in

speaker: EDGAR
text: stealth, wolf in greediness, dog in madness, lion in

speaker: EDGAR
text: prey. Let not the creaking of shoes nor the rustling

speaker: EDGAR
text: of silks betray thy poor heart to woman. Keep thy

speaker: EDGAR
text: foot out of brothels, thy hand out of plackets, thy

speaker: EDGAR
text: pen from lenders’ books, and defy the foul fiend.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Still through the hawthorn blows the cold wind;

speaker: EDGAR
text: says suum, mun, nonny. Dolphin my boy, boy, sessa!

speaker: EDGAR
text: Let him trot by.

speaker: LEAR
text: Thou wert better in a grave than to answer with

speaker: LEAR
text: thy uncovered body this extremity of the skies.—Is

speaker: LEAR
text: man no more than this? Consider him well.—Thou

speaker: LEAR
text: ow’st the worm no silk, the beast no hide, the sheep

speaker: LEAR
text: no wool, the cat no perfume. Ha, here’s three on ’s

speaker: LEAR
text: are sophisticated. Thou art the thing itself; unaccommodated

speaker: LEAR
text: man is no more but such a poor, bare,

speaker: LEAR
text: forked animal as thou art. Off, off, you lendings!

speaker: LEAR
text: Come, unbutton here.

speaker: FOOL
text: Prithee, nuncle, be contented. ’Tis a naughty

speaker: FOOL
text: night to swim in. Now, a little fire in a wild field

speaker: FOOL
text: were like an old lecher’s heart—a small spark, all

speaker: FOOL
text: the rest on ’s body cold.

speaker: FOOL
text: Look, here comes a walking fire.

speaker: EDGAR
text: This is the foul fiend Flibbertigibbet. He begins

speaker: EDGAR
text: at curfew and walks till the first cock. He

speaker: EDGAR
text: gives the web and the pin, squints the eye, and

speaker: EDGAR
text: makes the harelip, mildews the white wheat, and

speaker: EDGAR
text: hurts the poor creature of earth.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Swithold footed thrice the ’old,

speaker: EDGAR
text: He met the nightmare and her ninefold,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Bid her alight,

speaker: EDGAR
text: And her troth plight,

speaker: EDGAR
text: And aroint thee, witch, aroint thee.

speaker: KENT
text: How fares your Grace?

speaker: LEAR
text: What’s he?

speaker: KENT
text: Who’s there? What is ’t you seek?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What are you there? Your names?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Poor Tom, that eats the swimming frog, the

speaker: EDGAR
text: toad, the tadpole, the wall newt, and the water;

speaker: EDGAR
text: that, in the fury of his heart, when the foul fiend

speaker: EDGAR
text: rages, eats cow dung for sallets, swallows the old

speaker: EDGAR
text: rat and the ditch-dog, drinks the green mantle of

speaker: EDGAR
text: the standing pool; who is whipped from tithing to

speaker: EDGAR
text: tithing, and stocked, punished, and imprisoned;

speaker: EDGAR
text: who hath had three suits to his back, six shirts to

speaker: EDGAR
text: his body,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Horse to ride, and weapon to wear;

speaker: EDGAR
text: But mice and rats and such small deer

speaker: EDGAR
text: Have been Tom’s food for seven long year.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Beware my follower. Peace, Smulkin! Peace, thou

speaker: EDGAR
text: fiend!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What, hath your Grace no better company?

speaker: EDGAR
text: The Prince of Darkness is a gentleman. Modo

speaker: EDGAR
text: he’s called, and Mahu.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Our flesh and blood, my lord, is grown so vile

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: That it doth hate what gets it.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Poor Tom’s a-cold.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Go in with me. My duty cannot suffer

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: T’ obey in all your daughters’ hard commands.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Though their injunction be to bar my doors

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And let this tyrannous night take hold upon you,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Yet have I ventured to come seek you out

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And bring you where both fire and food is ready.

speaker: LEAR
text: First let me talk with this philosopher.

speaker: LEAR
text:
To
                 
                Edgar
                .
                 
              What is the cause of thunder?

speaker: KENT
text: Good my lord, take his offer; go into th’ house.

speaker: LEAR
text: I’ll talk a word with this same learnèd Theban.—

speaker: LEAR
text: What is your study?

speaker: EDGAR
text: How to prevent the fiend and to kill vermin.

speaker: LEAR
text: Let me ask you one word in private.

speaker: KENT
text: Importune him once more to go, my lord.

speaker: KENT
text: His wits begin t’ unsettle.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Canst thou blame him?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: His daughters seek his death. Ah, that good Kent!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: He said it would be thus, poor banished man.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thou sayest the King grows mad; I’ll tell thee, friend,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I am almost mad myself. I had a son,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Now outlawed from my blood. He sought my life

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But lately, very late. I loved him, friend,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: No father his son dearer. True to tell thee,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The grief hath crazed my wits. What a night’s this!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: —I do beseech your Grace—

speaker: LEAR
text: O, cry you mercy, sir.

speaker: LEAR
text:
To
                 
                Edgar
                .
                 
              Noble philosopher, your company.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Tom’s a-cold.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: In fellow, there, into th’ hovel. Keep thee warm.

speaker: LEAR
text: Come, let’s in all.

speaker: KENT
text: This way, my lord.

speaker: LEAR
text: With him.

speaker: LEAR
text: I will keep still with my philosopher.

speaker: KENT
text: Good my lord, soothe him. Let him take the fellow.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Take him you on.

speaker: KENT
text: Sirrah, come on: go along with us.

speaker: LEAR
text: Come, good Athenian.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: No words, no words. Hush.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Child Rowland to the dark tower came.

speaker: EDGAR
text: His word was still “Fie, foh, and fum,

speaker: EDGAR
text: I smell the blood of a British man.”
